title: Anatomy of the main() Function in C
description: A flowchart showing the structure, arguments, and return process of the main function, the entry point of a C program in UNIX.
relationships: The operating system calls main() with three arguments
argc contains the count of command-line arguments
argv is an array of strings containing the arguments
envp is an array of strings containing the environment variables
Program logic executes based on the provided arguments
A return value of 0 signals successful execution to the OS
A non-zero return value signals an error to the OS
